title: Helianthus microclinus
intro: Helianthus microclinus, also known as the little sunflower or dwarf sunflower, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with various conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Helianthus microclinus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Helianthus microclinus is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Helianthus microclinus is a species of sunflower. It is classified as a member of the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it within the order Asterales and the subclass Rosidae. Helianthus microclinus is further classified as a genus within the tribe Heliantheae. Its classification also includes being a part of the subfamily Helianthoideae.
